intro.subtitle: The Spirit of Rapa Nui Lives Here
intro.welcomeWords: Welcome · Bienvenue · Willkommen · Bienvenido · Vítejte · Witamy
intro.tagline: Experience the Spirit of Rapa Nui

audio.retry: Click again to start the ocean sound.

login.access: Access is reserved for invited viewers with a password.
login.confidentiality: I confirm that the materials presented on this website are confidential and undertake not to copy, distribute or share them with third parties without the project author’s written consent.
login.rulesText: The materials presented on this website form the author’s concept for Ahu Tongariki Europe and are confidential. Access has been granted solely to selected recipients for project review. Without prior written consent, copying, distributing, sharing, publishing or using any part of the materials, images, visualisations or descriptions is prohibited.
login.confirmRequired: Please confirm the confidentiality terms to access the presentation.

hero.title: Ahu Tongariki Europe
hero.subtitle: The world’s first full-scale Ahu Tongariki inspired monument outside Easter Island.
hero.imageAlt: Visualisation of fifteen Moai statues on a monumental stone platform

slogan.description: Ahu Tongariki Europe is a concept for creating a full-scale reconstruction in Poland of one of the world’s most recognisable archaeological ensembles. The project brings together education, culture, tourism and respect for Rapa Nui heritage.
slogan.imageAlt: Moai facing the panorama of Bystrzyca Kłodzka at sunset

project.title: A place where architectural scale meets education, landscape and cultural memory.
project.body: Ahu Tongariki Europe is a concept for creating in Poland a full-scale reconstruction of Ahu Tongariki, one of the world’s most recognisable archaeological ensembles from Rapa Nui. The plan envisages 15 Moai arranged in a composition inspired by the original, with respect for the cultural heritage of Easter Island.

iorana.title: IORANA MOAI
iorana.intro: This part of the project is conceived as a tribute to the makers of the Moai and to the heritage of Rapa Nui. Its purpose is to share knowledge, invite deeper engagement with the story of Ahu Tongariki and build a thoughtful bridge between Europe and Easter Island.
iorana.imageAlt: Museum-style visualisation of Rano Raraku with an unfinished Moai held in the rock
iorana.imageBody: The great unfinished Moai in the slopes of Rano Raraku speaks of ambition, technical mastery and the spiritual dimension of the work of the island’s early inhabitants. Here, the visualisation serves reflection, education and a respectful invitation to discover the authentic history of Rapa Nui.
themes: A story of ancestral knowledge, stone craft and Rano Raraku as the birthplace of many statues.
An educational perspective grounded in respect for research, documentation and the authentic history of Ahu complexes.
The European concept is intended to bring Rapa Nui heritage to a wider audience and deepen curiosity about its sources.
iorana.dialogueBody: “IORANA MOAI” does not treat Rapa Nui culture as decoration. It is an invitation to encounter its history, achievements and place in world heritage with care, and with respect for the community whose memory and traditions stand behind this story.

statues.imageAlt: Architectural detail of the Moai alignment
statues.imageCaption: A sequence of fifteen figures read as a horizontal line in the landscape.
statues.title: Statue layout – a faithful copy from Rapa Nui
statues.intro: The planned composition is based on the clear axis of the Ahu platform, a sequence of fifteen figures and the characteristic rhythm known from the 1995 reconstruction of Ahu Tongariki.
facts: The statue with the red head covering (Pukao)
layout inspired by the Ahu Tongariki reconstruction

location.mapAlt: Map of Europe highlighting Poland and the project location in the south-west
location.mapsAria: Open the Ahu Tongariki Europe project location in Google Maps
location.mapBody: A point in south-western Poland, close to the Czech border.
location.title: South-western Poland as the centre of gravity for a planned cultural attraction.
location.intro: The concept locates the complex in Bystrzyca Kłodzka, Lower Silesia, with a viewing relationship to the town and the Kłodzko Valley.
location.quote: The statues are intended to face Bystrzyca Kłodzka, creating a relationship between landscape, heritage and a new tourism function for the place.
location.openMap:
Click the Ahu Tongariki miniature
to open the location in Google Maps

functions.title: A programme designed as a coherent cultural and tourism destination.
functions.intro: The functions are shaped around longer visits, education, visitor services and the creation of new value for the region.
items: A monumental stone platform axis with fifteen statues.
Programmes on Rapa Nui heritage, archaeology and cultural preservation.
Temporary exhibitions, models, multimedia and the story of the reconstruction process.
Guest facilities rooted in the offer of the region.
A dialogue with the Kłodzko Valley landscape and the panorama of Bystrzyca.
Intimate concerts, talks, lectures and partnership programmes.

night.title: NIGHTS OF AHU TONGARIKI
night.subtitle: When stone awakens to life.
night.body:
As the sun sets, Ahu Tongariki Europe reveals a second identity.

The monumental silhouettes of the Moai emerge from darkness through carefully designed illumination, creating a space of silence, reflection and awe.

Evening light presentations, night viewing sessions, astronomy encounters and intimate cultural events turn each night into a distinct experience.
night.imageAlt: Night illumination of fifteen Moai statues on the Ahu Tongariki Europe platform
night.panelTitle: Light that draws history from stone.
night.panelBody: The lighting is intended to guide the eye across facial features, stone texture and the rhythm of the entire platform, creating an experience that is calm, dignified and far removed from amusement-park spectacle.
cards: A daily light experience after sunset, emphasising the monumental presence of the statues.
A quiet zone for contemplation and observation of the Bystrzyca Kłodzka panorama.
Evenings with telescopes, sky observation and the Milky Way.
Acoustic music, historical narration, educational meetings and cultural gatherings.
night.finalWhite: From the first rays of sun to a night illuminated by stars.
night.finalGold: Ahu Tongariki Europe reveals its beauty anew at every hour of the day.

regional.title: A project with economic, promotional and educational momentum.
regional.intro: The planned reconstruction can work for the region throughout the year: as a destination, a reason to stay longer and a recognisable symbol of a new tourism offer.
items: promotion of Bystrzyca Kłodzka and Lower Silesia

partnership.imageAlt: A monumental Moai visual used as the background for the Rapa Nui partnership section.
partnership.title: Partnership with Rapa Nui
partnership.body: The project should be developed with respect for Rapa Nui cultural heritage and in dialogue with official representatives of the island, the local community and institutions dedicated to heritage protection.

technical.title: Scale parameters relevant to planning and investment discussions.
technical.intro: The data organises the spatial assumptions of the concept and helps assess the potential of the complex in Bystrzyca Kłodzka.
